summary: The backend follows a classic layered architecture built on Spring Boot, exposing REST APIs for a rich legal document and deed management domain. It implements a broad set of capabilities—from CRUD actions and key‑management to archiving and scheduled jobs—while integrating with relational databases, external KMS, and authorization services. Quality analysis shows moderate separation of concerns, coupling, and cohesion, with a C grade reflecting architectural debt such as thin or God controllers/services and an anemic domain model. Targeted refactoring toward clearer service interfaces, stronger domain‑driven design, and tighter layer boundaries will raise maintainability and scalability.

capabilities: Create, read, update, and delete actions
Persist and manage deed entries, including connections, logs and locks
Handle document metadata storage and retrieval
Perform handover data‑set management and finalization
Execute archiving operations and sign submission/re‑encryption tokens
Manage cryptographic keys, re‑encryption state and key‑manager queries
Provide business‑purpose lookup and validation services
Schedule and run background jobs (e.g., re‑encryption, cleanup)
Expose REST APIs for all domain operations
Integrate with external authentication/authorization services via JSON auth
Apply correction notes and change tracking
functional.bounded_context: Legal Document & Deed Management with Archiving and Cryptographic Key Handling

debt_indicators: Large number of controllers and services (potentially thin or God controllers/services)
High entity count suggests possible anemic domain model and tight coupling to persistence
Missing clear service interfaces for many implementations
Potential business logic leakage into controller layer
recommendations: Introduce service interfaces and depend on abstractions rather than concrete implementations
Consolidate related controllers into cohesive modules or group by bounded context
Refactor oversized services into smaller, focused components
Apply domain‑driven design principles to move business logic into domain layer and reduce anemic entities
Add architectural guidelines to enforce layer boundaries and reduce cross‑layer dependencies

overall_assessment: The backend is functionally comprehensive and technically aligned with modern Spring‑based practices, but architectural quality suffers from moderate coupling and anemic domain modeling. The current design permits rapid feature addition but hampers long‑term maintainability. Addressing the identified debt—especially by introducing clear abstractions, tightening layer boundaries, and enriching the domain layer—will significantly improve cohesion, reduce coupling, and elevate the overall grade.
top_recommendations: Introduce service interfaces and depend on abstractions rather than concrete implementations
Apply domain‑driven design principles to move business logic into the domain layer and reduce anemic entities
Refactor oversized services into smaller, focused components
